content.full_text:
KAY CENICEROS
3rd District Supervisor
County of Riverside
Craig A Manning, Administrative Assistant
Melba Hoffman, Secretary

3rd District

August 28, 1990

Mr. Scott Hallabrin
Acting General Counsel
Fair Political Practices Commission
P. O. Box 807
Sacramento, CA 95804

Dear Mr. Hallabrin:

As a member of the Riverside County Board of Supervisors,
I have been offered an opportunity to have a letter from
me included in a welcoming packet to be sent to new
residents in the Hemet area of my District. This project
has been developed by private enterprise and, while I would
not be charged, participating merchants pay for having
information included in packet.

The purpose of my letter would be to make newcomers aware
of county services, facilities and opportunities to become
involved in their new community. The letter will be sent,
at the expense of the packet's developers, to about 1200
new residents each quarter.

I would like to participate and pay the cost of printing
my letter from campaign funds. However, I do not intend
it as a campaign activity and I am requesting a written
FPPC opinion of whether or not it would be considered a
campaign mailing.

Thank you for your consideration.

Sincerely,

Kay Ceniceros
Supervisor
Third District

KC:jlm:fg

4080 Lemon Street, Fourteenth Floor • Riverside, California 92501 • 714-787-2630

sections.question: Would including a letter from me in a welcoming packet to be sent to new residents in the Hemet area, paid for from campaign funds, be considered a campaign mailing?
sections.question_synthetic: A Riverside County Supervisor seeks FPPC guidance on whether a letter to new residents in her district, to be included in a privately-developed welcoming packet and paid for with campaign funds, would constitute a campaign mailing under FPPC regulations.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: This is an incoming request letter from a government official to the FPPC, not an FPPC advice letter response. No QUESTION or CONCLUSION sections are formally marked. The question is extracted from the body of the letter. No FPPC response is included in this document.

This project has been developed by private enterprise and, while I would not be charged, participating merchants pay for having information included in packet. The purpose of my letter would be to make newcomers aware of county services, facilities and opportunities to become involved in their new community. The letter will be sent, at the expense of the packet's developers, to about 1200 new residents each quarter. I would like to participate and pay the cost of printing my letter from campaign funds. However, I do not intend it as a campaign activity and I am requesting a written FPPC opinion of whether or not it would be considered a campaign mailing. Thank you for your consideration.
